title: Cerastium glandulosum
taxonomy: Cerastium glandulosum is classified within the family Caryophyllaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Cerastium, which includes approximately 40 species. Cerastium glandulosum is a flowering plant native to North America. It is a type of annual or perennial herb, and its classification also includes it as a member of the order Caryophyllales. As a member of the family Caryophyllaceae, it is closely related to other flowering plants such as Silene and Arenaria.
uses: Cerastium glandulosum is a lesser-known species, but its medicinal uses have been studied. Cerastium glandulosum has been found to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help with reducing pain and swelling. The plant's extracts have also been shown to have antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage. Additionally, Cerastium glandulosum has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever and respiratory issues. However, more research is needed to fully understand and confirm its medicinal uses.

intro: Cerastium glandulosum, also known as sticky mouse-ear or glandular mouse-ear, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). The health benefits of Cerastium glandulosum include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to its ability to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Cerastium glandulosum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including skin issues, digestive problems, and respiratory issues.
